question_text: Which of the following best describes the principle of conservation of energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy can be created when you burn fuel or use electricity.

options.label: B
options.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed; it can only be transferred from one form to another.

options.label: C
options.text: Energy is lost as heat whenever anything moves.

options.label: D
options.text: Energy can only exist in the form of kinetic or potential energy.

explanation: The conservation of energy principle is fundamental: energy cannot be made or destroyed, only transferred between forms. Option A is wrong because burning fuel transfers chemical energy, not creates it. Option C is wrong because heat is a form of energy transfer, not energy loss. Option D is wrong because energy has many forms (electrical, sound, thermal, etc.).
